11	/**
12	 * Mandatory math formatting instructions injected into every chat prompt.
13	 * Ensures the LLM always uses LaTeX delimiters so the frontend can render math properly.
14	 */
15	const MATH_FORMATTING_INSTRUCTIONS = `## Math Formatting (CRITICAL)
16	
17	Always use LaTeX delimiters for mathematical expressions:
18	- Inline math (within sentences): \\(...\\)
19	- Block/display math (standalone equations): \\[...\\]
20	
21	IMPORTANT: Never use dollar signs ($) for math delimiters. Always use \\(...\\) for inline math and \\[...\\] for block math.
22	
23	Never write math as plain text. Use proper LaTeX notation for fractions (\\frac{}{}), multiplication (\\cdot), square roots (\\sqrt{}), trigonometric functions (\\sin, \\cos, \\tan), Greek letters (\\alpha, \\pi), etc.
24	
25	When referencing exercise content, always wrap mathematical expressions in \\(...\\) or \\[...\\] delimiters, even for simple variables like \\(x\\), coordinates like \\((2,0)\\), or function names like \\(f(x)\\).
26	
27	## Response Format (CRITICAL)
28	
29	NEVER output raw JSON, code blocks, or structured data in your responses. Always respond in natural language with proper markdown formatting. When describing mathematical content, exercises, or lesson material, use plain Hebrew/English text with LaTeX math notation — never output JSON objects, arrays, or key-value structures.
30	
31	## Markdown Structure (CRITICAL)
32	
33	NEVER use more than 2 spaces of indentation in your markdown. Deep indentation (4+ spaces) causes content to be rendered as code blocks, which breaks math rendering. Use flat list structures:
34	- Use \`-\` or \`*\` for bullet points, with at most one level of nesting
35	- For nested content, use bold headers or line breaks instead of deep indentation
36	- NEVER indent continuation text with 4 or more spaces — it will be treated as a code block and math will not render`

38	/**
39	 * Mandatory image handling instructions injected into every chat prompt.
40	 * Ensures the LLM provides clear, actionable feedback when it cannot process an uploaded image.
41	 */
42	const IMAGE_HANDLING_INSTRUCTIONS = `## Image Handling (CRITICAL)
43	
44	When a student uploads an image, you MUST check ALL of the following conditions BEFORE attempting to help with the content. Do NOT skip any check even if you can partially read the exercise:
45	
46	1. **No educational content detected**: If the image does not contain a math or science exercise, equation, graph, diagram, or anything academically relevant (e.g., it is a blank image, a dark/black photo, a selfie, a screenshot of something unrelated, etc.), you MUST:
47	   - Explain what is wrong with the image (e.g., "התמונה כהה לחלוטין", "זו לא נראית כמו תמונה של תרגיל").
48	   - Tell the student what a good image looks like (e.g., "אנא העלה תמונה ברורה ומוארת של התרגיל, וודא שהטקסט והמספרים קריאים").
49	
50	2. **Cropped or cut off (IMPORTANT — check this every time)**: Look carefully at ALL edges of the image. If ANY text or content is cut off at the left, right, top, or bottom edge — even if you can still read most of the exercise — you MUST tell the student the image is cropped before helping. Describe which part is cut off (e.g., "הטקסט חתוך בצד שמאל — חלק מהמילים והמספרים חסרים"). Ask them to re-upload the full, uncropped exercise. Do NOT attempt to solve a cropped exercise — the missing parts may change the meaning of the problem.
51	
52	3. **Unreadable / low quality**: If the image seems to contain an exercise but is blurry, too dark, too bright, overexposed, rotated, or the text/numbers are not legible, tell the student exactly what is wrong and how to fix it (e.g., better lighting, hold camera steady, avoid glare, rotate the image).
53	
54	4. **Too small to read**: If the content is too tiny to make out, ask the student to upload a larger or higher-resolution version, or to zoom in on the specific exercise.
55	
56	5. **Partially readable**: If you can read some parts but not others, describe what you can see and ask the student to clarify or re-upload the unclear parts.
57	
58	6. **Upside down or rotated**: If the image is rotated or upside down, tell the student and ask them to re-upload it in the correct orientation.
59	
60	7. **Multiple exercises**: If the image contains multiple exercises, ask the student which one they need help with, or ask them to upload a photo of just the specific exercise.
61	
62	8. **Supported formats**: Only JPEG, PNG, WebP images and PDF files are accepted. Maximum file size is 20 MB. Images must be at least 100×100 pixels. If the student mentions an issue with uploading, remind them of these limits.
63	
64	9. **Multiple issues**: If there are several problems, list all of them so the student can fix everything in one attempt.
65	
66	IMPORTANT:
67	- Always respond in the SAME LANGUAGE the student used. If the student writes in Hebrew, you MUST respond in Hebrew. If in English, respond in English.
68	- Always explain what is wrong with the image AND how to take a better one.
69	- When rejecting an image for ANY of the reasons above (rules 1-9), you MUST include the exact tag [IMAGE_REJECTED] at the very end of your response. This tag is a rejection signal used by the system; the tag itself is stripped from what the student sees. Do NOT include this tag when the image is acceptable and you are helping with the exercise.`

174	/**
175	 * Format exercise content blocks into readable text for the system prompt.
176	 * Extracts key information from content blocks to give the LLM context.
177	 */
178	function formatExerciseContent(content: unknown): string {
179	  if (!content || typeof content !== 'object') return '(No content)'
180	
181	  const data = content as { blocks?: unknown[] }
182	  if (!Array.isArray(data.blocks) || data.blocks.length === 0) return '(No content)'
183	
184	  const parts: string[] = []
185	
186	  for (const block of data.blocks) {
187	    if (!block || typeof block !== 'object') continue
188	
189	    const b = block as { type?: string; value?: string; prompt?: unknown }
190	
191	    if (b.type === 'latex') {
192	      const latexBlock = b as { type: 'latex'; latex: string }
193	      parts.push(`[LaTeX]: ${latexBlock.latex}`)
194	    } else if (b.type === 'rich_text') {
195	      const rtBlock = b as { type: 'rich_text'; value: string }
196	      if (rtBlock.value) parts.push(rtBlock.value)
197	    } else if (b.type === 'question_select') {
198	      const qBlock = b as { type: 'question_select'; prompt?: { value?: string } }
199	      const prompt =
200	        qBlock.prompt && typeof qBlock.prompt === 'object'
201	          ? (qBlock.prompt as { value?: string }).value
202	          : undefined
203	      if (prompt) parts.push(`[Question]: ${prompt}`)
204	    } else if (b.type === 'question_free_response') {
205	      const qBlock = b as { type: 'question_free_response'; prompt?: { value?: string } }
206	      const prompt =
207	        qBlock.prompt && typeof qBlock.prompt === 'object'
208	          ? (qBlock.prompt as { value?: string }).value
209	          : undefined
210	      if (prompt) parts.push(`[Free Response Question]: ${prompt}`)
211	    } else if (b.type === 'question_table') {
212	      const qBlock = b as { type: 'question_table'; prompt?: { value?: string } }
213	      const prompt =
214	        qBlock.prompt && typeof qBlock.prompt === 'object'
215	          ? (qBlock.prompt as { value?: string }).value
216	          : undefined
217	      if (prompt) parts.push(`[Table Question]: ${prompt}`)
218	    } else if (b.type === 'question_matching') {
219	      const qBlock = b as { type: 'question_matching'; prompt?: { value?: string } }
220	      const prompt =
221	        qBlock.prompt && typeof qBlock.prompt === 'object'
222	          ? (qBlock.prompt as { value?: string }).value
223	          : undefined
224	      if (prompt) parts.push(`[Matching Question]: ${prompt}`)
225	    } else if (b.type === 'html') {
226	      const htmlBlock = b as { type: 'html'; html?: string }
227	      if (htmlBlock.html) {
228	        // Strip HTML tags for a cleaner text representation
229	        const text = htmlBlock.html
230	          .replace(/<[^>]+>/g, ' ')
231	          .replace(/\s+/g, ' ')
232	          .trim()
233	        if (text) parts.push(`[Content]: ${text}`)
234	      }
235	    } else if (b.type === 'svg') {
236	      const svgBlock = b as { type: 'svg'; caption?: { value?: string } }
237	      const caption =
238	        svgBlock.caption && typeof svgBlock.caption === 'object'
239	          ? (svgBlock.caption as { value?: string }).value
240	          : undefined
241	      if (caption) parts.push(`[SVG Diagram]: ${caption}`)
242	    }
243	    // Skip geometry, axis, multi_axis blocks (visual content not easily represented in text)
244	  }
245	
246	  return parts.length > 0 ? parts.join('\n') : '(No extractable text content)'
247	}

33	/**
34	 * Pull readable text out of an exercise's `content.blocks[]` shape.
35	 * Handles two block flavours seen in the schema:
36	 *  - `rich_text` blocks: text under `.value`
37	 *  - `question_*` blocks (e.g. `question_geometry`): `.prompt` / `.hint` on the
38	 *    block itself, no `.value`. Without this branch, multi-part exercises
39	 *    surface only their intro paragraph and the model has no specific
40	 *    sub-question to ground answers in.
41	 *
42	 * Truncated to 4 KB to keep the system prompt bounded.
43	 */
44	/**
45	 * Coerce a field that the schema models as InlineRichTextSchema (an object
46	 * `{type:'rich_text', value: string, ...}`) but might also appear as a raw
47	 * string in older or inline data, into a plain string suitable for the
48	 * system prompt. Returns undefined when no usable text is found.
49	 */
50	function richTextToString(value: unknown): string | undefined {
51	  if (!value) return undefined
52	  if (typeof value === 'string') {
53	    const trimmed = value.trim()
54	    return trimmed.length > 0 ? trimmed : undefined
55	  }
56	  if (typeof value === 'object') {
57	    const v = (value as { value?: unknown }).value
58	    if (typeof v === 'string') {
59	      const trimmed = v.trim()
60	      return trimmed.length > 0 ? trimmed : undefined
61	    }
62	  }
63	  return undefined
64	}
65	
66	/**
67	 * Pull readable text out of an exercise's `content.blocks[]` shape.
68	 * Handles two block flavours seen in the schema:
69	 *  - `rich_text` blocks: text under `.value`
70	 *  - `question_*` blocks (e.g. `question_geometry`): `.prompt` / `.hint` on the
71	 *    block itself. In production these are InlineRichText objects (not plain
72	 *    strings) — the helper above unwraps them. Without this branch,
73	 *    multi-part exercises surface only their intro paragraph and the model
74	 *    has no specific sub-question to ground answers in.
75	 *
76	 * Truncated to 4 KB to keep the system prompt bounded.
77	 */
78	function extractExerciseBody(content: unknown): string | undefined {
79	  if (!content) return undefined
80	  // Some setups store content as a JSON-encoded string
81	  let normalized: unknown = content
82	  if (typeof normalized === 'string') {
83	    try {
84	      normalized = JSON.parse(normalized)
85	    } catch {
86	      return undefined
87	    }
88	  }
89	  if (typeof normalized !== 'object' || normalized === null) return undefined
90	  const blocks = (normalized as { blocks?: unknown[] }).blocks
91	  if (!Array.isArray(blocks) || blocks.length === 0) return undefined
92	
93	  const parts: string[] = []
94	  blocks.forEach((block, idx) => {
95	    if (!block || typeof block !== 'object') return
96	    const b = block as Record<string, unknown>
97	    const type = (b.type as string | undefined) ?? 'block'
98	
99	    // 1) Rich-text intro / explanation block (standalone rich_text)
100	    if (type === 'rich_text') {
101	      const text = richTextToString(b)
102	      if (text) {
103	        parts.push(text)
104	        return
105	      }
106	    }
107	
108	    // 2) Question-style blocks carry prompt/hint as InlineRichText fields
109	    const prompt = richTextToString(b.prompt)
110	    const hint = richTextToString(b.hint)
111	    if (prompt || hint) {
112	      const sub: string[] = [`### Sub-question ${idx + 1} (${type})`]
113	      if (prompt) sub.push(`Prompt: ${prompt}`)
114	      if (hint) sub.push(`Hint (do not reveal directly; use for guidance): ${hint}`)
115	      parts.push(sub.join('\n'))
116	      return
117	    }
118	
119	    // 3) Unknown / opaque block — leave a marker so the model knows something is there
120	    parts.push(`[${type} block]`)
121	  })
122	
123	  if (parts.length === 0) return undefined
124	  const joined = parts.join('\n\n').trim()
125	  if (!joined) return undefined
126	  const MAX = 4000
127	  return joined.length > MAX ? joined.slice(0, MAX) + '\n…(truncated)' : joined
128	}

130	/**
131	 * Build a markdown block describing the current lesson and (optionally) exercise
132	 * so the model always knows what the student is working on, even when no admin
133	 * Prompt is linked to the lesson.
134	 *
135	 * Fields are looked up tolerantly — missing values are simply skipped.
136	 */
137	function buildLessonContextBlock(
138	  lesson?: Record<string, unknown> | null,
139	  chapter?: Record<string, unknown> | null,
140	  course?: Record<string, unknown> | null,
141	  exercise?: Record<string, unknown> | null,
142	): string | undefined {
143	  const lines: string[] = []
144	
145	  const lessonTitle = lesson?.title as string | undefined
146	  const lessonType = lesson?.type as string | undefined
147	  const chapterTitle = chapter?.title as string | undefined
148	  const courseTitle = course?.title as string | undefined
149	
150	  if (lessonTitle || chapterTitle || courseTitle) {
151	    lines.push('## Current Lesson')
152	    if (courseTitle) lines.push(`Course: ${courseTitle}`)
153	    if (chapterTitle) lines.push(`Chapter: ${chapterTitle}`)
154	    if (lessonTitle) lines.push(`Lesson: ${lessonTitle}`)
155	    if (lessonType) lines.push(`Type: ${lessonType}`)
156	  }
157	
158	  if (exercise) {
159	    const exTitle = exercise.title as string | undefined
160	    // V1 schema (legacy): top-level prompt/hint
161	    const exPromptLegacy = exercise.prompt as string | undefined
162	    const exHintLegacy = exercise.hint as string | undefined
163	    // Current schema: rich-text blocks under exercise.content.blocks[]
164	    const exBody = extractExerciseBody(exercise.content)
165	
166	    if (exTitle || exPromptLegacy || exHintLegacy || exBody) {
167	      if (lines.length > 0) lines.push('')
168	      lines.push('## Current Exercise')
169	      if (exTitle) lines.push(`Title: ${exTitle}`)
170	      if (exPromptLegacy) lines.push(`Prompt: ${exPromptLegacy}`)
171	      if (exHintLegacy) {
172	        lines.push(`Hint (do not reveal directly; use for guidance): ${exHintLegacy}`)
173	      }
174	      if (exBody) {
175	        lines.push('Body:')
176	        lines.push(exBody)
177	      }
178	    }
179	  }
180	
181	  if (lines.length === 0) return undefined
182	  return [
183	    'The following describes what the student is currently working on. Use it to ground your responses; do not refuse to discuss it.',
184	    '',
185	    ...lines,
186	  ].join('\n')
187	}

102	      hidden: extras?.hidden === true,
103	      media: extras?.media,
104	      chatAssets: extras?.chatAssets,

109	export async function agentChat(req: PayloadRequest & { json?: () => Promise<unknown> }) {
110	  const requestId = crypto.randomUUID()
111	  const reqLogger = logger.child({ requestId })
112	
113	  let guestSession: Awaited<ReturnType<typeof getGuestSessionByToken>> | null = null
114	  let isGuestMode = false
115	  let guestCookieHeader: string | undefined
116	
117	  // 1) Auth - check for authenticated user OR guest session
118	  // req.user is set by Payload middleware for real HTTP requests.
119	  // Fall back to payload.auth() for cases where middleware hasn't run.
120	  let user = req.user
121	  if (!user) {
122	    const authResult = await req.payload.auth({ headers: req.headers })
123	    user = authResult.user
124	  }
125	
126	  if (!user) {
127	    // Check for guest session
128	    const guestToken = getGuestSessionCookie(req.headers as unknown as Headers)
129	    if (guestToken) {
130	      guestSession = await getGuestSessionByToken(req.payload, guestToken)
131	    }
132	
133	    if (!guestSession) {
134	      // Create new guest session
135	      const ipHash = hashIP(req.headers?.get('x-forwarded-for') || req.headers?.get('x-real-ip'))
136	      const userAgentHash = hashUserAgent(req.headers?.get('user-agent'))
137	
138	      // Check rate limit for new guests
139	      const rateLimitResult = await checkRateLimit(ipHash, userAgentHash)
140	      if (!rateLimitResult.allowed) {
141	        return Response.json(
142	          {
143	            error: 'Too many requests. Please try again later.',
144	            isGuestMode: true,
145	            retryAfter: Math.ceil((rateLimitResult.resetAt - Date.now()) / 1000),
146	          },
147	          {
148	            status: 429,
149	            headers: {
150	              'Retry-After': String(Math.ceil((rateLimitResult.resetAt - Date.now()) / 1000)),
151	              'X-RateLimit-Remaining': String(rateLimitResult.remaining),
152	              'X-RateLimit-Reset': String(rateLimitResult.resetAt),
153	            },
154	          },
155	        )
156	      }
157	
158	      const { session, token } = await createGuestSession(req.payload, {
159	        ipHash,
160	        userAgentHash,
161	      })
162	      guestSession = session
163	      isGuestMode = true
164	
165	      guestCookieHeader = await buildGuestSessionCookieHeader(token)
166	    } else {
167	      isGuestMode = true
168	    }
169	  }
170	
171	  // No auth at all - reject
172	  if (!user && !guestSession) {
173	    return Response.json(
174	      { error: 'Authentication or guest session required', isGuestMode: false },
175	      { status: 401 },
176	    )
177	  }
178	
179	  if (!user && guestSession) {
180	    reqLogger.info({ guestSessionId: guestSession.id }, 'Processing guest chat request')
181	
182	    const messageLimit = await checkAndIncrementGuestMessageCount(req.payload, guestSession.id)
183	    if (!messageLimit.allowed) {
184	      return Response.json(
185	        {
186	          error: 'Guest message limit reached. Sign up for unlimited access.',
187	          isGuestMode: true,
188	          retryAfter: null,
189	        },
190	        {
191	          status: 429,
192	          headers: {
193	            'X-Guest-Message-Limit': 'true',
194	          },
195	        },
196	      )
197	    }
198	  }
199	
200	  try {
201	    // 2) Parse and validate request body
202	    if (!req.json) {
203	      return Response.json({ error: 'Invalid request' }, { status: 400 })
204	    }
205	
206	    const parseResult = await parseRequestBody(req.json.bind(req))
207	    if (!parseResult.success) {
208	      return Response.json(
209	        { error: 'Invalid request', details: parseResult.error.issues },
210	        { status: 400 },
211	      )
212	    }
213	
214	    const validated = parseResult.data
215	
216	    // Safely extract user info (may be null for guests)
217	    const userId = user?.id
218	    const userRole = isUsersCollectionUser(user)
219	      ? ((user as unknown as { role: AccountRole }).role as AccountRole)
220	      : AccountRole.Student
221	    const isAdmin = userRole === AccountRole.Admin
222	
223	    // Check authenticated user chat quota (skip for admins, guests, and hidden messages)
224	    if (userId && !isAdmin && !validated.hidden) {
225	      const quota = await checkAndIncrementChatQuota(req.payload, userId)
226	      if (!quota.allowed) {
227	        return Response.json(
228	          {
229	            error: 'Chat limit reached. Try again later.',
230	            quotaExceeded: true,
231	            questionsUsed: quota.questionsUsed,
232	            maxQuestions: quota.maxQuestions,
233	            resetAt: quota.resetAt,
234	          },
235	          { status: 429 },
236	        )
237	      }
238	    }
239	
240	    // 3) Check if admin mode (guests cannot use admin mode)
241	    const adminMode = isAdmin && validated.adminMode === true
242	
243	    // For admin mode, check if we have a context or adminMode
244	    const contextCandidate = extractContextCandidate(validated)
245	
246	    // Admin mode without context - use admin:user:{userId} context
247	    if (adminMode && !contextCandidate) {
248	      return handleAdminModeChat(req, requestId, validated, reqLogger)
249	    }
250	
251	    // Regular mode - context required
252	    if (!contextCandidate) {
253	      return Response.json(
254	        { error: 'Missing context ID (requires exerciseId, lessonId, chapterId, or courseId)' },
255	        { status: 400 },
256	      )
257	    }
258	
259	    // Continue with regular context-scoped chat...
260	    return handleContextScopedChat(
261	      req,
262	      requestId,
263	      validated,
264	      contextCandidate,
265	      reqLogger,
266	      userId,
267	      guestSession?.id,
268	      isGuestMode,
269	      guestCookieHeader,
270	    )
271	  } catch (error) {
272	    // Handle connection reset errors gracefully
273	    if (
274	      error &&
275	      typeof error === 'object' &&
276	      'code' in error &&
277	      (error as { code: string }).code === 'ECONNRESET'
278	    ) {
279	      reqLogger.debug({ err: error }, 'Client disconnected during chat request')
280	      return Response.json({ error: 'Request cancelled' }, { status: 499 })
281	    }
282	
283	    reqLogger.error({ err: error }, 'Chat endpoint error')
284	
285	    if (error instanceof z.ZodError) {
286	      return Response.json({ error: 'Invalid request', details: error.issues }, { status: 400 })
287	    }
288	
289	    return Response.json({ error: 'Internal server error' }, { status: 500 })
290	  }
291	}

33	/**
34	 * Pull readable text out of an exercise's `content.blocks[]` shape.
35	 * Handles two block flavours seen in the schema:
36	 *  - `rich_text` blocks: text under `.value`
37	 *  - `question_*` blocks (e.g. `question_geometry`): `.prompt` / `.hint` on the
38	 *    block itself, no `.value`. Without this branch, multi-part exercises
39	 *    surface only their intro paragraph and the model has no specific
40	 *    sub-question to ground answers in.
41	 *
42	 * Truncated to 4 KB to keep the system prompt bounded.
43	 */
44	/**
45	 * Coerce a field that the schema models as InlineRichTextSchema (an object
46	 * `{type:'rich_text', value: string, ...}`) but might also appear as a raw
47	 * string in older or inline data, into a plain string suitable for the
48	 * system prompt. Returns undefined when no usable text is found.
49	 */
50	function richTextToString(value: unknown): string | undefined {
51	  if (!value) return undefined
52	  if (typeof value === 'string') {
53	    const trimmed = value.trim()
54	    return trimmed.length > 0 ? trimmed : undefined
55	  }
56	  if (typeof value === 'object') {
57	    const v = (value as { value?: unknown }).value
58	    if (typeof v === 'string') {
59	      const trimmed = v.trim()
60	      return trimmed.length > 0 ? trimmed : undefined
61	    }
62	  }
63	  return undefined
64	}
65	
66	/**
67	 * Pull readable text out of an exercise's `content.blocks[]` shape.
68	 * Handles two block flavours seen in the schema:
69	 *  - `rich_text` blocks: text under `.value`
70	 *  - `question_*` blocks (e.g. `question_geometry`): `.prompt` / `.hint` on the
71	 *    block itself. In production these are InlineRichText objects (not plain
72	 *    strings) — the helper above unwraps them. Without this branch,
73	 *    multi-part exercises surface only their intro paragraph and the model
74	 *    has no specific sub-question to ground answers in.
75	 *
76	 * Truncated to 4 KB to keep the system prompt bounded.
77	 */
78	function extractExerciseBody(content: unknown): string | undefined {
79	  if (!content) return undefined
80	  // Some setups store content as a JSON-encoded string
81	  let normalized: unknown = content
82	  if (typeof normalized === 'string') {
83	    try {
84	      normalized = JSON.parse(normalized)
85	    } catch {
86	      return undefined
87	    }
88	  }
89	  if (typeof normalized !== 'object' || normalized === null) return undefined
90	  const blocks = (normalized as { blocks?: unknown[] }).blocks
91	  if (!Array.isArray(blocks) || blocks.length === 0) return undefined
92	
93	  const parts: string[] = []
94	  blocks.forEach((block, idx) => {
95	    if (!block || typeof block !== 'object') return
96	    const b = block as Record<string, unknown>
97	    const type = (b.type as string | undefined) ?? 'block'
98	
99	    // 1) Rich-text intro / explanation block (standalone rich_text)
100	    if (type === 'rich_text') {
101	      const text = richTextToString(b)
102	      if (text) {
103	        parts.push(text)
104	        return
105	      }
106	    }
107	
108	    // 2) Question-style blocks carry prompt/hint as InlineRichText fields
109	    const prompt = richTextToString(b.prompt)
110	    const hint = richTextToString(b.hint)
111	    if (prompt || hint) {
112	      const sub: string[] = [`### Sub-question ${idx + 1} (${type})`]
113	      if (prompt) sub.push(`Prompt: ${prompt}`)
114	      if (hint) sub.push(`Hint (do not reveal directly; use for guidance): ${hint}`)
115	      parts.push(sub.join('\n'))
116	      return
117	    }
118	
119	    // 3) Unknown / opaque block — leave a marker so the model knows something is there
120	    parts.push(`[${type} block]`)
121	  })
122	
123	  if (parts.length === 0) return undefined
124	  const joined = parts.join('\n\n').trim()
125	  if (!joined) return undefined
126	  const MAX = 4000
127	  return joined.length > MAX ? joined.slice(0, MAX) + '\n…(truncated)' : joined
128	}
